title: Is there a way to dependency inject modules in C++
body:
Currently I implement a C++ harness that handles the top-level module signals. For instance, I can mock(emulate) an external SRAM in C++.

However, I would like to replace some of the lower level modules with C++ implementation too. These are lower level modules that have no top-level input/output signals. This can be useful to improve simulation speed, but also for debugging purposes.

My first instinct was to specify a top level module, then simply delete the Verilog modules that I want to implement in C++ in the hopes that Verilator would generate everything for the Verilog and that I would implement the rest in C++ myself.

If Verilator does not provide this option, then my next step would be to try to see if I can get Chisel to generate Verilog where the modules which I want to impelement in C++ have their signals exported to the top level module, in which case implementing the C++ version of those modules(mocking or emulating them in C++) is straightforward: just like emulating an external SRAM for instance.

If I delete a sumodule, I get the expected error message:


```
%Error: Foo.v:1018:3: Cannot find file containing module: 'Bar'
```
